title: eria reptans var. matsudae
intro: Eria reptans var. matsudae, also known as Yamamahigo, is a type of orchid native to Asia, particularly in the mountainous regions of Japan. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eria reptans var. matsudae include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or powder, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
intro_study: According to "Frontiers in nutrition", eria reptans var. matsudae, being a species of Eryngium, has been studied for its beneficial properties in treating diabetes and dyslipidemia, with its bioactive components showing potential in improving glucose metabolism and reducing serum concentrations of lipids and cholesterol.
